Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

This patch failed its own test:

 Cache used before read: 161324 KiB
 CMD: trevis-20vm7 cat /proc/meminfo | grep ^Cached:
 Cache used after read: 161284 KiB
 cannot give advice: Unknown error 524 (524)
 ladvise: cannot give advice 'dontneed' to file '/mnt/lustre/f255b.sanity': Unknown error 524
 sanity test_255b: @@@@@@ FAIL: Ladvise dontneed again failed

Patch Set 4:

Hmm, Andreas, did you run the test yourself or it was done by Maloo? Would you please share the link if Maloo? Because I can't find any failure here: https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/a221e150-3376-11e6-bbf5-5254006e85c2

Patch Set 4:

It was from the sanity test_255b failure above:

https://testing.hpdd.intel.com/test_sets/502a7604-338b-11e6-a0ce-5254006e85c2

Patch Set 4:

I suspect the problem is that the failure was using osd-zfs, but this patch only implements dontneed for osd-ldiskfs.

Maybe Alex can provide some info on how to implement the cache flush for osd-zfs?

Patch Set 4:

Yeah, that was the cause of failure. And if Alex is able to help, that would be great. Otherwise, I will need to skip the test suite for ZFS. The willread patch didn't fail on ZFS test, because the advice was hanlded on OFD level, and never passed to OSD level. But we still need to look into the willread support for ZFS to make sure it does the correct thing.

parents.subject: LU-930 misc: update README path to git hooks

commit.subject: LU-4931 ladvise: Add dontneed advice support for ladvise
commit.message:
LU-4931 ladvise: Add dontneed advice support for ladvise

This patch addds DONTNEED advice to ladvise framework. OSS will
cleanup the page cache of the file when this hint is provided.

Signed-off-by: Li Xi <lixi@ddn.com>
Signed-off-by: Gu Zheng <gzheng@ddn.com>
Change-Id: If5cf7f3193924ca7cccb96d8d841c0d889469393
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/20203
Reviewed-by: Wang Shilong <wshilong@ddn.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
